stories.headline: Anthropic launches Claude Tag to track AI activity across your company
stories.blurb: Claude Tag lets you add a tracking snippet to your app so you can see which teams use Claude, what prompts they run, and how much it costs. Works across API, web, and mobile — giving admins a single dashboard for usage, spend, and compliance.
stories.why_it_matters: Finally a way to know who's actually using Claude and what they're building with it.

stories.headline: Meta just launched smart glasses without Ray-Ban
stories.blurb: After three years of Ray-Ban exclusivity, Meta is shipping its own-brand smart glasses in three styles and seven colors. The Verge got hands-on with the new frames, which drop the Ray-Ban partnership but keep Meta's AI features. One style is a collab with a celebrity influencer.
stories.why_it_matters: Meta's betting it can sell smart glasses on AI alone, not just fashion branding.
stories.original_headline: Meta launches cheaper smart glasses without Ray-Ban

stories.headline: Groq raised $650M and hired new execs after Nvidia bought its team for $20B
stories.blurb: The AI chip startup confirmed a new funding round and executive hires after Nvidia paid $20 billion mostly for Groq's engineering talent. Groq is now focusing on its neocloud business—letting customers rent its inference chips instead of buying hardware.
stories.why_it_matters: A startup can survive losing its entire team if the tech and business model still work
stories.original_headline: AI chipmaker Groq confirms $650M raise, re-staffs after Nvidia’s $20B not-acqui-hire deal

stories.headline: Google DeepMind just paid A24 $75M to build AI filmmaking tools
stories.blurb: Google DeepMind and indie studio A24 (Everything Everywhere All at Once, The Whale) are partnering to develop AI tools for film production. The $75 million deal marks one of the biggest bets yet on AI entering Hollywood's creative pipeline.
stories.why_it_matters: Major studios are now paying real money to figure out where AI fits in actual filmmaking.
stories.original_headline: Google DeepMind bets $75M on AI’s future in Hollywood with A24 deal

try_this_prompt.title: The negotiation flip
try_this_prompt.prompt: I'm preparing for a negotiation I'll describe below. Don't tell me what to ask for. Instead: tell me what the other side actually wants that I haven't thought about, what I could offer that costs me little but they'd value highly, and the one concession I should refuse even if it feels small.
try_this_prompt.tool_hint: Before salary talks, vendor deals, or any conversation where you're about to anchor too early.

daily_question: If your company tracked every prompt you sent to Claude this week, what would be the most embarrassing one?

args.reason: Lead story: Major Anthropic product launch. Claude Tag brings AI as a persistent team member in Slack - concrete, shippable, and represents a new interaction paradigm. Primary source (Anthropic News). High try-it-soon appeal for Solvd teams.
